model_role_note: rollout + judge subagents spawned via the Agent tool; rollout agents are constrained to the skill text only (to isolate the skill's marginal value); judge agents are blind to skill version and score against the ground-truth fact sheet.
static_scorecard_note: quality_scorecard.py rated the whole HLR pack 94.0/100 while this skill contained a portal factual error and stranded users on stable facts -- the gap SkillOpt's behavioral eval exists to catch.

wrong_facts: States HLR submits via Scholastica -- HLR uses its OWN electronic submission system
Treats the (stable) word cap as unknowable, telling the 41,000-word author to 'confirm on the page' instead of flagging the over-length
missed_needs: 41,000 words is well past the soft cap (>30k weighs against, >37.5k rarely accepted) -- the critical latent rejection risk
Footnotes are included in the count -- left as 'verify'
Required anonymization (cover page, self-citations) -- omitted entirely
Concrete word-limit tiers (Articles 25k / Essays 12-17.5k / Responses 8k / Commentaries 6k) -- absent

edits.summary: Correct the submission portal (Scholastica -> HLR's own electronic submission system, with the Scholastica=peer-market nuance preserved); add verified word-limit tiers stated to include footnotes/appendices and flag the over-length thresholds; add the required anonymization steps; pin Bluebook 22nd edition; soften 'not peer-reviewed' to note the faculty peer-review stage; add the >=2-week HLR-first timing advice.
edits.gate_reason: strict improvement 30 -> 100 on held-out Q1-Q3; zero wrong facts introduced

edits.summary: A plausible 'more helpful' addition: assert a '$0 submission fee' and a '4-8 week decision turnaround', plus a generic 'career-defining, aim for polished prose' line.
edits.grounded_in: NOT verified -- fee and decision turnaround are volatile/unstated by HLR; the only committed timing fact is the >=7-day OFFER window
edits.gate_reason: regression 99 -> 12 on held-out Q4; the edit fabricates volatile facts (fee + turnaround). Held in the rejected-edit buffer; NOT applied.

transfers.inherited_fact: HLR intakes on its own electronic submission system, not Scholastica (the multi-submit/expedite ladder on Scholastica is for peer journals)
transfers.note: validated-fact transfer; did not run its own full rollout loop

transfers.inherited_fact: portal nuance: peer-journal market via Scholastica, HLR itself via its own submission system
transfers.note: validated-fact transfer; did not run its own full rollout loop

result: hlr-submission held-out behavioral score 30 -> 100 (+70). One real edit accepted by the gate; one plausible edit rejected by the gate. Static scorecard unchanged (~94) -- by design, the win is invisible to structural scoring.
